methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Thea Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Brian Berzin that has raised $20M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient narrow comparables. Only one same-stage and one adjacent-stage peer existed within the nuclear energy cohort, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median-based inference. A stage-progression approach was therefore applied: using total raised ($20M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the next stage, yielding an implied value of $40M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $34M (low) to $46M (high), with a median of $40M. This single-method triangulation reflects reliance on stage-based progression mechanics rather than peer multiples, introducing material uncertainty around appropriate step-up calibration for frontier nuclear technology. Validation would benefit from: (1) expanded comparable set as more Series B nuclear companies report valuations; (2) revenue or reactor-advancement metrics if available; and (3) sector-specific risk adjustment for regulatory and deployment timelines. The wide range reflects these methodological constraints.
